originalTitle: Fears for press freedom as billionaire takes control of East Africa's largest media house
title: Big News for East Africa: Who Tells Our Stories?
content:
Jambo, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly news reporter, here to tell you about something super important happening right here in East Africa. Get ready to learn about news, truth, and why it all matters to you and your family! Sometimes, very big things happen in our world, and we need to understand them. Today, we're talking about a very rich man and a very important way we get our news. It might sound a bit grown-up, but I promise to explain it in a way that's easy to understand.

A Very Big News Family!

Imagine a giant factory, but instead of making toys or clothes, it makes news! This factory has many different parts. It has a TV studio where presenters share stories. It has a radio station where you can listen to music and news. It has people who write newspapers, filled with words and pictures. This big news factory is called a 'media house.' And guess what? There's a super-duper big one right here in East Africa! It's one of the largest places that makes and shares news all across our beautiful region. It sends out stories every single day to millions of people – your parents, your teachers, maybe even you! This media house has been telling stories for a very long time. It helps people know what's happening in their town, in their country, and all over Africa. It's like a special messenger that brings important information right to our homes. Did you know? The very first newspaper was printed over 400 years ago! That's a super long time!

Meet Mr. Rostam Aziz

Now, let's talk about a man named Mr. Rostam Aziz. He is a very, very rich man. When we say someone is a 'billionaire,' it means they have an amazing amount of money. Imagine having so many coins that you couldn't count them all even if you tried all day and all night! That's how much money a billionaire has. Mr. Aziz is a famous businessman. He has many businesses, and he likes to make things happen. Recently, he did something really big. He bought this super-duper large news factory, the biggest media house in East Africa! This means that now, he owns the TV stations, the radio stations, and the newspapers that belong to this big company. It's like he bought the whole team that makes the news.

Why is This a Big Deal?

Think about telling a story to your friends. You want to tell the truth, right? You want to make sure everyone hears what really happened. News reporters are like super storytellers, but their most important job is to always tell the truth. When a very rich person buys a big news company, some people start to wonder. They ask, "Will the news stories still be fair and truthful? Or will the new owner want the news to say only certain things?" This is where an important idea comes in, called 'press freedom.' It means that people who write and share the news should be free to tell the truth, even if it's a difficult truth. They should not be told what to say by anyone, especially not by the person who owns the company. Did you know? In many countries, it's against the law to tell lies in the news, because telling the truth is so important!

What is 'Journalistic Independence'?

Let's imagine you are a super detective, a journalist! Your job is to find out what really happened. You ask questions, you talk to people, and you look for clues. You want to tell everyone the full story, not just part of it. 'Journalistic independence' means that you, the reporter, can do your job freely. No one should tell you to hide a clue or to make up a part of the story. You should be independent, which means you are free to follow the facts wherever they lead. If the owner of the news company has very strong ideas about something, some people worry that reporters might feel they have to write stories that match the owner's ideas, instead of just the truth. This is why people are talking a lot about Mr. Aziz buying the big media house. Reporters want to be sure they can keep telling stories fairly and honestly, without anyone else telling them what to say or not to say. They want to be independent.

Why Do We Need News That is Free and Fair?

Free and fair news is like a strong light that helps us see clearly. It helps everyone in East Africa know what is really happening in our communities. When we know the truth, we can make good choices. For example, if there's a new school being built, or a new rule about clean water, the news helps us understand it. If the news only tells one side of the story, or hides important information, then people might not know the whole truth. News also helps people talk to their leaders. If people see something that needs to be better, they can learn about it from the news and then share their ideas. It helps our communities grow strong and fair. Did you know? People who report the news are sometimes called 'the watchdogs' because they watch out for important things happening in our world!

What Happens Next?

Right now, people in East Africa and all around the world are watching to see what happens. Will the news stories stay fair? Will the reporters still be free to tell the whole truth, just like before? It's like a big test for everyone. The reporters want to keep doing their best work. The people who read and watch the news want to keep trusting what they hear. Everyone hopes that the news will continue to be a voice for all people, not just for one person or one idea.

What Can YOU Do?

Even though you are kids, you can do something very important! You can be curious! Always ask questions. If you hear a story, think about it. Ask, "Is this the whole story?" "Are there other ways to see this?" Listen to your parents and teachers. Read different books and look at different sources of information when you are older. Be like a little detective, always looking for the truth! Your voice matters, and your ability to think for yourself is super powerful. Keep learning, keep asking questions, and keep being amazing! The future of East Africa is in your hands, and understanding how news works is a big part of that. Keep shining brightly, little stars of Africa!
summary: A very rich man in East Africa bought the biggest news company, making people wonder if the news stories will still be fair and truthful, which is called 'press freedom' and 'journalistic independence'.
whyMatters: It matters because news helps everyone know what's happening and make good choices, so it's super important that the news always tells the whole truth, fairly, and freely, for all the people in East Africa.

title: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
content: The move forms part of a broader process of returning cultural artefacts to African countries that started in 2017.‎

originalTitle: Ghana star to deny rape charges, UK court hears
title: A Famous Ghanaian Footballer and a Court Story
content:
Hello, Super Friends from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's me, your friendly news reporter for Africa. I have an important story to share with you today. It's about a famous football player from Ghana. His name is Thomas Partey. He plays for a very big football team far away in a country called the UK. Many of you might know him because he is a big star! He makes Ghana and all of Africa very proud when he plays. Football is a game where two teams try to kick a ball into a net. It is a very popular game all over the world. Thomas Partey is very good at it!

Who is Thomas Partey?

Thomas Partey is a hero to many children and adults. He comes from Ghana, a beautiful country in West Africa. He plays in the middle of the field, helping his team score goals and stopping the other team from scoring. He plays for a famous team called Arsenal. It is one of the biggest teams in the world! When he plays for Ghana, he wears the national colours, making his country proud. He has many fans who cheer for him. Many kids dream of being a football star just like him one day. Did you know? Ghana is famous for its delicious cocoa, which is used to make chocolate! It is also known for its gold.

What is This Story About?

Well, something very serious has happened. Someone has said that Thomas Partey did something very, very wrong to a woman a long time ago. This was in December of 2020. That means it happened more than three years ago. When someone says another person did something wrong, we call it a 'charge'. It means a serious claim has been made against them. It is not a small thing. It is a very big and important claim. But here is the very important part: Thomas Partey says he did NOT do it. He says he is innocent. This means he believes he has done nothing wrong. He wants to show everyone that the claim is not true. He wants to clear his name. This is a very important part of the story.

What is a Court?

Because this is such a serious claim, Thomas Partey will have to go to a special place. This place is called a 'court'. A court is like a big, official room where people go to find out the truth when there are big problems. It is a very serious place. In the court, there are special people called judges. The judge listens very carefully to everyone. They help to make sure things are fair for everyone. The court's job is to find out what really happened. It is like a big puzzle the court needs to solve. Did you know? Courts are very old. They have been around for hundreds of years! They help keep rules and make sure everyone is safe.

Going to Court to Find the Truth

Thomas Partey will go to a court in the UK next month. This is where he will tell his side of the story. The court will listen to what the person who made the claim says. They will also listen to what Thomas Partey says. They will look at all the facts. They will look at any evidence. Evidence is like clues that help find the truth. It is very important to look at all the clues. This process takes time. It is not like a quick football game. It can take many days or even weeks. The court wants to be very sure about what happened. It wants to be fair to everyone involved.

Why is 'Denying' Important?

When Thomas Partey says he will 'deny' the charges, it means he is strongly saying he did not do it. This is very important. In many countries, there is a very important rule. This rule is called 'innocent until proven guilty'. What does that mean? It means we must think that someone is good and has not done anything wrong until the court has shown proof that they did. We must not just believe a claim without waiting for the truth to come out. It is like when you play a game. If someone says you cheated, you have a chance to say you did not. Then, everyone looks at the game to see what really happened. The court is like the person who watches the game to make sure it is fair.

Waiting for Fairness and Truth

So, what do we do now? We wait. We wait for the court to do its job. It is very important for us to be fair. We should not make up our minds before the court does. We should let the judge and the court find all the facts. Everyone deserves to be treated fairly. Even famous football stars like Thomas Partey. And the woman who made the claim also deserves for the truth to be found. Did you know? Justice means making sure things are fair for everyone. It means that rules are followed and people are treated right.

A Serious Time

This is a very serious time for Thomas Partey. It is also serious for the woman who made the claim. The court will work hard to understand everything. We hope that the truth will come out. And we hope that whatever the court decides, it will be fair and right. Let's send good wishes for fairness and truth as this story continues. We will keep you updated when the court has made its decision. Thank you for listening, my super friends!
summary: Ghanaian football star Thomas Partey will go to court in the UK next month because someone says he did something very serious, but he says he did not do it.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows that everyone, even famous football players, must follow the law and go to court if a serious claim is made, and everyone is innocent until a court shows proof.
